prompt: What system control should you adjust to compensate for sound attenuation with increasing depth?
What system control determines the amount of amplification that occurs in the receiver?
When you adjust the output power control, you affect the following system component:
What system control do you adjust to equalize the differences in echo amplitudes received from similar structures situated at different depths?
Electronic noise is reduced in the ultrasound system by this method:
You have chosen to enlarge an ultrasound image with a read magnification. Which of the following is NOT related to read magnification?
What term below describes the rate at which the transmitter applies electronic voltage pulses to the transducer?
Pulsing of the transmitted sound wave is necessary for real-time imaging because:
choices: The transducer becomes too hot to handle if continuous sound waves are emitted.
The crystal in the transducer will break under the stress of continuous emissions.
The depth of the interface from which the echo originated can be determined.
Lateral resolution is improved by pulsed transmission.
Temporal resolution is improved by pulsed transmission.

prompt: What receiver function is responsible for decreasing the difference between the smallest and largest received signal amplitudes?
What receiver function listed below is NOT operator-adjustable?
While performing a sonographic examination, you have performed both preprocessing and postprocessing functions. Which of the following functions is postprocessing?
Which of the following would be most helpful to enhance the contrast difference between tissues having subtle variations in echogenicity?
choices: Performing a read-zoom magnification over the area of interest

prompt: What part of the sonographic instrument is responsible for apodization, beam steering, focusing, and aperture control?
What control should you adjust to better compensate for the attenuation of sound as it propagates through tissue?
Which control would you adjust to increase the intensity of the transmitted pulse?
Which control would you adjust to alter the dynamic range of the displayed echoes?
You are performing a sonographic examination and select the tissue harmonics operating mode. What advantage will you obtain over conventional imaging?
You have decreased the scan line density. What technique will be employed to fill in the empty data between the scan lines?
What is a typical frame rate for B-mode real-time imaging?
You have increased the output power by 10 dB. The signal intensity has:
What is the purpose of the preamplification of the incoming signal that occurs in the transducer assembly?
choices: To decrease the dynamic range of the signal
To increase echo voltages before noise is induced through the cable
To reduce sensitivity to side lobes
To reduce acoustic impedance mismatch between the tissue and the transducer
To compensate for attenuation of the beam with depth

prompt: What function of the receiver converts electric signals from radiofrequency to video form?
What component of the ultrasound system converts the electric energy provided by the pulser into acoustic pulses transmitted into the patient?
The incoming raw echo signals must be compressed into a smaller dynamic range because:
choices: A wide dynamic range results in increased display of electronic noise.
A wide dynamic range does not allow differentiation between echoes arriving from different depths.
The display cannot accommodate the wide dynamic range of the incoming signals.
The existing wide dynamic range significantly slows the frame rate.
A wide dynamic range compromises spatial resolution.

prompt: Which of the following is NOT a function of the receiver?
What system function is limited by the speed of sound in tissue?
What control should you adjust to modify the image if the attenuation coefficient of the tissue is very high?
You made changes to the following controls during an exam. Which adjustment increased the duty factor?
What is the advantage of using write-zoom instead of read-zoom magnification?
choices: Write zoom always provides a higher frame rate than read zoom.
Write zoom allows you to select a location in the image, whereas read zoom must be applied to only the center of the image.
Write zoom reduces the display of electronic noise and side lobes because it is performed in the memory component of the instrument.
Write zoom provides greater dynamic range than read zoom.
Write zoom provides better spatial resolution than read zoom.

prompt: How is reflector depth determined with pulse-echo ultrasound?
What effect will you detect in the image if you increase the threshold control?
choices: Decreased appearance of weak echo signals
Increased appearance of strong echo signals
Decreased appearance of strong echo signals

prompt: What can you do to improve the temporal resolution while scanning?
choices: Increase the number of focal zones
Decrease the acoustic scan line density

prompt: The number of images displayed per second in real-time imaging is termed the:
Which scanning mode does NOT rely on the principle of echo ranging to determine interface location?
Which display mode may be used to calculate distance measurements?
Which of the following best describes M-mode?
choices: Presentation of reflector amplitudes along multiple lines of sight
Movement of a single structure along multiple lines of sight
Depth of reflectors along a single line of sight vs. time
Frequency shift of moving reflectors along a single line of sight

prompt: Which is an assumption of sonographic imaging that determines how detected echoes are mapped in the image?
choices: Sound waves travel along curved paths.
Echoes originate from the most recently transmitted pulse.
Large structures yield a high brightness level on the display.
Media with varying propagation speeds alter the travel time of the sound pulse.

prompt: What physical concept allows us to determine the location of an interface by measuring the elapsed time between the transmitted pulsed ultrasound wave and the detected echo from that interface?
The elapsed time between the transmitted pulse and the detected echo is 26 microseconds. How far is the interface from the transducer?
During a sonographic exam, you adjusted the following controls. Which one was most likely to have affected the frame rate?
Which of the following does NOT affect the time required to create one image frame?
Increasing the number of acoustic scan lines in one frame improves which of the following?
You have decreased the image sector width. Which of the following will most likely change as a result of this adjustment?
Which of the following ultrasound examinations would require the highest frame rate?
Which of the following adjustments would NOT decrease frame rate?
choices: Increasing the number of transmit focal zones

prompt: When you increase the scanning depth, which of the following is automatically decreased by the system to avoid range ambiguity?
You are scanning an interface with a high acoustic impedance mismatch. How will this be displayed on the B-mode image?
choices: The height of the spike or brightness of the dot is not affected by acoustic impedance mismatch.

prompt: The 'A' in A-mode stands for:
The 'M' in M-mode stands for:
What component is necessary for a real-time B-mode scanner but is not present in an A-mode scanner?
What system control converts the numbers stored in memory to proportional voltages that control the brightness on a CRT monitor?
A digital scan converter is another name for what system component?
What control allows the user to freeze and then scroll back through the most recently acquired image frames?
A group of 8 bits is called:
Where are images stored in the ultrasound system?
What is the maximum number of shades of gray in an 8-bit digital scan converter?
The spatial resolution of the scan converter is determined by:
choices: Number of pixels in the scan converter matrix
Brightness and contrast settings of the monitor

prompt: What system component is most responsible for transmit focusing?
How many levels of information can a single bit represent?
The picture elements in a scan converter matrix are termed:
Digital-to-analog conversion of the ultrasound signal must occur at what part of the imaging process?
choices: Between the transducer and the receiver
Between the beam former and the transducer
Between the receiver and the scan converter
Between the scan converter and the display
Digital-to-analog conversion does not occur in the ultrasound system.

prompt: In the scan converter, interpolation is performed to:
choices: Amplify the reflected signal in comparison to the background noise
Decrease the dynamic range to a level that can be handled by the monitor
Amplify weak echo signals from deep structures to compensate for beam attenuation
Fill in the empty pixels that occur between acoustic scan lines
Increase the number of shades of gray in the resultant image

prompt: Without interpolation, which of the following would best describe the resultant B-mode image?
choices: Very weak echoes in the far field compared to the near field
Poor differentiation between the ultrasound signal and the noise floor
Saturation of the white shades on the ultrasound monitor
A series of scan lines with blank data between the lines

prompt: What three colors are used on a color monitor to produce the range of available colors?
When you 'freeze' the ultrasound image, where is the echo information stored?
What type of resolution is determined by the number of pixels in the scan converter?
Which of the following is associated with improper adjustment of the brightness and contrast on the video monitor?
choices: The spatial resolution of the resultant image will be degraded.
The operator will likely overgain or undergain the image.
The temporal resolution will be reduced.
The flicker rate of the video monitor will be increased.
The penetration of the transducer will be reduced.

prompt: What can you improve by changing the gray-scale map assignment?
Which of the following controls most directly affects patient exposure?
Which of the following adjustments would increase the duty factor?
Which of the following best describes the relationship between frame rate and temporal resolution?
choices: Higher frame rate improves temporal resolution
Lower frame rate improves temporal resolution
Frame rate does not affect temporal resolution
Temporal resolution depends only on axial resolution
Temporal resolution depends only on lateral resolution

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve contrast resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of bits per pixel
Increasing the number of pixels per frame
Increasing the number of scan lines per frame
Increasing the number of focal zones

prompt: Which of the following controls would you adjust to reduce low-level echoes from the image?
Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve spatial resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of pixels per frame
Increasing the number of bits per pixel
Increasing the number of scan lines per frame
Increasing the number of focal zones

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve axial resolution?
Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve lateral resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of focal zones
Increasing the number of pixels per frame
Increasing the number of bits per pixel
Increasing the number of scan lines per frame

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve elevational resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of focal zones

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve temporal resolution?
choices: Decreasing the number of focal zones
Increasing the number of focal zones

Increasing the number of pixels per frame
Increasing the number of bits per pixel

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve contrast resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of bits per pixel
Increasing the number of pixels per frame
Increasing the number of scan lines per frame
Increasing the number of focal zones

prompt: Which of the following adjustments would most likely improve spatial resolution?
choices: Increasing the number of pixels per frame
